liberties.

classification.title: Constitution Of Kentucky 1891
classification.summary: This section of the Kentucky Constitution of 1891 outlines various limitations and powers related to taxation, debt assumption, and the use of the Commonwealth's credit. It specifies that the General Assembly cannot surrender the power to tax property, and the Commonwealth shall not assume debts of subdivisions unless for defense in war, invasion, or insurrection. The credit of the Commonwealth cannot be loaned to individuals or corporations, nor can it become a stockholder or make donations. Laws authorizing borrowing must specify the purpose, and borrowed money can only be used for that purpose. Counties, cities, or towns are restricted from becoming stockholders in corporations or loaning credit, except for bridge and road construction. The General Assembly may authorize poll taxes, and all levied taxes must specify their purpose.
